extraction_result.cfp_subject: Announcement of Opportunity for an Interdisciplinary Scientist and Guest Investigators in the BepiColombo mission. This AO solicits applications to augment the scientific return of the BepiColombo mission, a joint ESA and JAXA project with NASA participation, focusing on the detailed study of Mercury and its magnetosphere. The call aims to appoint up to one Interdisciplinary Scientist (IDS) from ESA Member States and up to eleven Guest Investigators (GIs), with specific allocations for ESA Member States, Japan, and the USA. Appointments are for a first period of three years, renewable, and are ad personam, with no funding provided by ESA for activities.

content.title: Formatting Requirements - Title only
subtitles: Proposal Submission Format: Only PDF files will be accepted. [Source 1, KC15]
Proposal File Protection: Files must not be locked or protected. [Source 1, KC15]
Proposal File Size Limit: Maximum 20 MB. [Source 1, KC15]

content.title: General Requirements - Title only
subtitles: Funding: ESA will not fund the activities of the IDS or the GIs; each proposer is responsible for securing their own funding. [Source 1, KC7]
Appointment Type: IDS or GI appointment is ad personam. [Source 1, KC7]
Appointment Duration: Appointments are for a first period of three years, renewable. [Source 1, KC7]
Interdisciplinary Scientist (IDS) Eligibility: Open to scientists only from ESA Member States. [Source 1, KC7]
Guest Investigator (GI) Eligibility: Open to scientists from ESA Member States, Japan, and USA. [Source 1, KC7]
Exclusions: Principal Investigators and Co-Principal Investigators of BepiColombo instrument teams are excluded. [Source 1, KC7]
Co-Investigator Eligibility: Eligible for IDS position, but not for GI positions. [Source 1, KC7]

content.title: Schedule - Title only
subtitles: Letters of Intent Due Date: 20 May 2025, 12:00 (noon) CEST. [Source 1, KC8]
Proposals Due Date: 17 June 2025, 12:00 (noon) CEST. [Source 1, KC8]
Appointment of the Interdisciplinary Scientist and Guest Investigators: Q4 2025. [Source 1, KC8]

content.title: Contact - Title only
subtitles: Yannis Zouganelis, Solar System Science Coordinator, Directorate of Science, ESA/ESAC - Madrid, Spain. [Source 1, KC9]
Yoshifumi Saito, Director, Department of Solar System Sciences, ISAS/JAXA. [Source 1, KC9]
Shoshana Z. Weider, Program Scientist, NASA Headquarters. [Source 1, KC9]
